elk: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Elkei.
ell: symbol. ISO 639-2 /T & ISO 639-3 language code for Greek.
ilk: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ilongot.
ill: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iranun.
ink: noun. A pigment (or dye)-based fluid used for writing, printing etc.
inky: adjective. Of the colour of ink, especially black ink; dark.
kelly: noun. A square or hexagonal pipe that is turned in order to rotate the rotary table of a drilling rig.
ken: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kenyang.
key: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kupia.
kill: verb. To put to death; to extinguish the life of.
kiln: noun. An oven or furnace or a heated chamber, for the purpose of hardening, burning, calcining or drying anything; for example, firing
kin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Kinyarwanda.
knell: verb. To ring a bell slowly, especially for a funeral; to toll.
kue: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kuman.
lei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lemio.
lek: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Leipon.
leu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kara (New Guinea).
ley: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lemolang.
lie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Likila.
like: verb. To enjoy, be pleased by; favor; be in favor of.
likely: adjective. Probable; having a greater-than-even chance of occurring.
liken: verb. Followed by to or (archaic) unto: to regard or state that (someone or something) is like another person or thing; to compare.
lily: noun. Any of several flowers in the genus Lilium of the family Liliaceae, which includes a great many ornamental species.
lin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Lingala.
line: noun. A path through two or more points (compare ‘segment’); a continuous mark, including as made by a pen; any path, curved or straight.
liney: adjective. Resembling or characterised by lines.
link: noun. A connection between places, people, events, things, or ideas.
linky: adjective. Of or pertaining to hyperlinks.
lune: noun. A fit of lunacy or madness; a period of frenzy; a crazy or unreasonable freak.
lunk: noun. A fool; an idiot; a lunkhead.
lye: noun. An alkaline liquid made by leaching ashes (usually wood ashes).
nil: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nila.
nill: verb. To be unwilling; will not (+ infinitive).
null: noun. A non-existent or empty value or set of values.
unlike: adjective. Not like; dissimilar (to); having no resemblance; unalike.
unlikely: adjective. Not likely; improbable; not to be reasonably expected.
yell: verb. To shout; holler; make a loud sound with the voice, especially to express intense emotions such as anger or excitement.
yen: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Yendang.
yin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yinchia.
yuk: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yuki.
yule: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Yule.
eli: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nding.
eyn: noun. Alternative form of eyen: plural of eye.
ike: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eastern Canadian Inuktitut.
kie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kibet.
kye: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Krache.
kyl: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kalapuya.
kyle: noun. A narrow arm or channel of the sea between an island and the mainland, or between two islands.
kyu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Western Kayah.
llyn: noun. An upland lake in Wales.
lue: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Luvale.
lunel: noun. A type of sweet muscat wine.
nek: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Neku.
nul: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nusa Laut.
nye: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nyengo.
ule: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lule.
ull: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ullatan.
uni: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Uni.
unkey: verb. To stop transmitting on a two-way radio, etc.
unkill: verb. To bring (something killed) back to life.
yike: verb. To utter the exclamation yikes.
yue: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cantonese.
